title: Why Infrared Sees Through Dust
script_summary: An exploration of how infrared wavelengths penetrate dust clouds, revealing hidden cosmic wonders.

segments.voiceover: Infrared wavelengths slip through dust clouds that block visible light.
In space, dust is everywhere. It hides stars, galaxies, and even entire star-forming regions.
Visible light gets absorbed or scattered, but infrared? It just passes through.
Telescopes like the JWST use infrared to unveil the cosmos, revealing what we can’t see with our eyes.
From ancient stellar nurseries to distant galaxies, infrared light expands our view of the universe.
Want to unlock more cosmic secrets? Follow us for more amazing astronomy insights!
